topic: What happens when a soil sensor fails (real troubleshooting)
voiceover_script: One morning, this bed wouldn't stop watering. Here's what actually happened. A soil sensor pulled halfway out of the ground reads bone dry — even sitting in a puddle, the system thinks it needs more water. The fix is always the same: re-seat the sensor properly in the soil, and check the cable for damage where it enters the ground. The other common failure is calibration drift — every sensor reads slightly differently, so dry and wet values get set per sensor, not assumed. Once you know the two or three things that actually go wrong, fixing this takes minutes, not an afternoon. The full troubleshooting guide — every real failure, symptom to fix — is in the build plans, link in bio.

The other common failure is calibration drift — every sensor reads slightly differently, so dry and wet values get set per sensor, not assumed.
Once you know the two or three things that actually go wrong, fixing this takes minutes, not an afternoon.
The full troubleshooting guide — every real failure, symptom to fix — is in the build plans, link in bio.
